title: Diospyros minutisepala
taxonomy: Diospyros minutisepala is classified as a species of evergreen tree in the family Ebenaceae, which is part of the order Ebenales. The genus Diospyros is commonly known as the persimmon or ebony tree family. Diospyros minutisepala is native to the tropical regions of Asia. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae, and is often found growing in low-lying areas or along rivers. The tree's classification is further supported by its unique characteristics, such as its small, yellowish fruits and dark brown bark.

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
